summary: Andrew Ng announces an experimental 'Agentic Reviewer' tool designed to provide faster feedback on research papers. Motivated by the painfully slow traditional peer-review process, he demonstrates that agentic workflows can approach human-level reviewing performance, particularly when grounded in open-access repositories like arXiv.

principles.statement: Fast feedback loops are critical for research iteration.
principles.rationale: Waiting months for traditional peer review feedback is painfully slow and stalls researchers' progress. Agentic workflows can solve this by providing rapid, actionable feedback.
principles.source_quote: Their feedback loop -- waiting ~6 months for feedback each time -- was painfully slow. We wanted to see if an agentic workflow can help researchers iterate faster.

principles.statement: Agentic AI is approaching human-level performance in complex evaluation tasks.
principles.rationale: When tested on ICLR 2025 reviews, the correlation between an AI and a human reviewer was slightly higher than the correlation between two human reviewers.
principles.source_quote: Correlation between two human reviewers: 0.41 - Correlation between AI and a human reviewer: 0.42 This suggests agentic reviewing is approaching human-level performance.

mental_models.name: Agentic workflows for iteration
mental_models.description: Using AI agents not just for generation, but as a rapid feedback mechanism to tighten iteration cycles in complex, traditionally slow processes.
mental_models.example: Using an AI agent to review research papers to avoid the 6-month wait time of traditional academic peer review.

heuristics: Use agentic workflows to tighten painfully slow feedback loops.
Ground AI agents in freely published domain data (like arXiv) to improve their performance and relevance.

signature_quotes.text: Their feedback loop -- waiting ~6 months for feedback each time -- was painfully slow. We wanted to see if an agentic workflow can help researchers iterate faster.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the motivation behind building an AI agent for reviewing research papers.

signature_quotes.text: This suggests agentic reviewing is approaching human-level performance.
signature_quotes.context: Comparing the Spearman correlation of AI-human reviews versus human-human reviews on ICLR 2025 data.

anti_patterns.description: Relying solely on traditional, slow feedback loops for iterating on complex work.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is 'painfully slow' and can result in a researcher spending years just waiting for feedback, severely bottlenecking their ability to improve and iterate.
